articles.title: BBC Knew About DJ Investigation Years Ago
articles.summary: The BBC, a big TV and radio company, knew about a police investigation into DJ Scott Mills in 2017. Scott Mills was a popular DJ on Radio 2. The police were looking into old claims of serious bad behavior from 1997 to 2000, but no charges were made because there wasn't enough evidence. Recently, the BBC got new information about Mills and fired him in March 2024. The BBC said they are working to improve how they handle such situations. Mills has not commented on this news. The BBC's boss at the time did not know about the investigation. This story shows how companies deal with problems and try to do the right thing.

articles.title: UK Plans to Work More with Europe Because of War
articles.summary: The UK's leader, Sir Keir Starmer, says the country will try to become closer friends with the European Union because of a war in Iran. This war is making things difficult for the UK, like higher prices for energy and fuel. Sir Keir wants to help people by making energy bills cheaper and paying workers more money. He says the UK has a plan to stay strong during this tough time. Other political groups have different ideas, such as removing taxes from energy bills or giving money to help with costs. The war might slow down the UK's economy, but the government is watching the situation and will help families if needed. Sir Keir is also meeting with many countries to talk about the war and how to fix problems like closed shipping routes.

articles.title: NASA's Moon Mission and Weather Check
articles.summary: NASA is getting ready to launch a special rocket called Artemis II to send astronauts around the Moon for the first time in over 50 years. The launch is planned from Florida at a place called Cape Canaveral, but the weather needs to be just right. If there are clouds, rain, strong winds, or lightning, NASA might have to delay the launch to keep everyone safe. A weather officer named Mark Burger is watching the sky closely to decide if it's safe to go. NASA says there's an 80% chance the weather will be good during the two-hour launch window. If not, they can try again on other days in early April. This mission is exciting because it helps us learn more about space!

articles.title: Estate Agents Say Rightmove Charges Too Much Money
articles.summary: Estate agents are upset because Rightmove, a big website for listing houses, is charging them very high fees. Some agents say the fees have more than doubled in recent years, and they are struggling to pay. A group of estate agents, led by an accountant named Jeremy Newman, is taking Rightmove to court. They claim Rightmove is using its strong position in the market to charge too much. Rightmove says the claim is not true and they will fight it. They believe they offer good value to agents and people looking for homes. Many agents feel they have to use Rightmove because it brings them most of their customers, even though it is expensive. This situation is causing agents to hire fewer people and provide less effective services.

articles.title: Democrats Try a New Way to Win in Utah
articles.summary: In Utah, a state where many people usually vote for Republicans, Democrats are trying a new idea to win a seat in the U.S. House of Representatives. They are running as progressives, which means they support big changes like higher wages and abortion rights. This is different from the past when Democrats tried to be moderate to get votes. The change happened because the voting districts were redrawn, creating a new area around Salt Lake City that has more Democratic voters. Ben McAdams, a former congressman, is running again and now supports progressive ideas, even though he used to be more conservative. He says he has experience getting things done. The Democratic Party is spending more money in Utah to help win this seat and build for the future. If a Democrat wins the primary election, they will likely win in November. This race is important because it could help Democrats take control of the House.

articles.title: President Trump and the Oil Problem with Iran
articles.summary: President Trump is upset because there is a war with Iran that might last a few more weeks. The Strait of Hormuz is a very important waterway where lots of oil ships travel, but Iran is blocking it, making oil and gas prices go up around the world. Trump is telling other countries to help by getting their own oil instead of relying on the U.S. The U.S. has attacked targets in Iran, like an ammunition depot, and Iran has attacked ships, including a Kuwaiti oil tanker. This is causing big problems for oil markets and making everyone worried about the cost of gas.
